Source_Species.reason: The paper is centered on TPS genes in Phoebe bournei and explicitly identifies TPS-a genes in this species.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Phoebe bournei is explicitly the study species throughout the paper.

Applied_Species.reason: All subfamily analyses, expression, and metabolite associations are in Phoebe bournei.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The paper analyzes VOCs in a sequenced tree and in 297 genotypes from 33 provenances grown in a common garden.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: TPS-a is explicitly one terpene synthase subfamily identified in P. bournei.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper states TPS-a predominantly comprises sesquiterpene synthases and links TPS-a to abundant sesquiterpenoids in P. bournei.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Sesquiterpenoids are explicitly described as terpenoids and the major products associated with TPS-a.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Sesquiterpenoids are reported as abundant in vegetative tissues, especially aboveground tissues, with stem bark at 96.17%.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Defense against pathogens/herbivores is stated generally for terpenoids and sesquiterpenoids, but wood durability is discussed more broadly for Phoebe wood and not directly demonstrated as a TPS-a-specific effect in Results.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results and Discussion state that more TPS-a copies were retained and constitutively expressed, corresponding to abundant sesquiterpenoid accumulation in P. bournei.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states most TPS-a genes were constitutively/highly expressed in stem and root tissues and more TPS-a copies were retained than TPS-b.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same species as source; same expression findings apply.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: TPS expression was examined across tissues and also under drought and cold stress for family-wide divergence.

Omics_Data.reason: BioProject PRJNA627308 for genome assembly and PRJNA628065 for RNA-seq are explicitly given; VOC profiling by GC-MS is described.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported: 34 TPS-a copies, retention/constitutive expression relative to TPS-b, and major contribution of TPS-a to abundant sesquiterpenoid accumulation and proposed pathogen resistance.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all explicitly used: genome-wide identification, phylogeny, tandem analysis, RNA-seq, GC-MS VOC profiling, and transient assays for representative TPS-a genes.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper reports positive selection on several TPS-a branches and presents the subfamily as central to the paper.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper suggests value for boosting plant immunity and selecting effective antipest/pathogen compounds, but 'prioritize TPS-a retention/stacking' is an extrapolative application not directly tested.

Source_Species.reason: TPS-b genes are identified and analyzed in Phoebe bournei.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Phoebe bournei is explicitly the species studied.

Applied_Species.reason: The claims are based on analyses in Phoebe bournei.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The VOC context includes the sequenced tree and 297 common-garden genotypes.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: TPS-b is explicitly defined as a terpene synthase subfamily in P. bournei.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper states TPS-b predominantly comprises monoterpene synthases.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Monoterpenoids are the product class associated with TPS-b.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Monoterpenoids are reported to accumulate significantly in root xylem, while being low in most other tissues.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Defense and scent are discussed for terpenoids broadly, but a direct TPS-b-specific functional role for monoterpenes in P. bournei Results is not demonstrated.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results explicitly say TPS-b also expanded by tandem duplication, but more copies were pseudogenized/lost and most TPS-b genes were underexpressed or condition-specific, matching low monoterpenoid abundance in most tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states half of TPS-b genes were underexpressed in all tissues analyzed and most TPS-b genes were expressed only under specific conditions or tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same species as source; same expression results apply.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: TPS family expression divergence was profiled across tissues and under drought/cold stress.

Omics_Data.reason: The cited genome and RNA-seq BioProjects and GC-MS VOC profiling are explicitly present.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The three summary points match the Results: 28 TPS-b copies, higher loss/pseudogenization with many fragments/pseudogenes, and contribution to low monoterpenoid abundance relative to sesquiterpenoids.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are explicitly used for TPS-b subfamily analyses.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly notes numerous TPS-b fragments and pseudogenes, indicating contraction relative to TPS-a.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Engineering/restoring TPS-b to shift flux toward monoterpenes is a plausible extrapolation but not directly tested in the paper.

Source_Species.reason: PbTPS-a21 is a gene from Phoebe bournei.

Applied_Species.reason: The gene originates from P. bournei and was functionally tested by transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both Phoebe bournei and Nicotiana benthamiana are explicitly mentioned.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The gene was cloned from the sequenced P. bournei tree; no variety is given for tobacco, so 'NA' is acceptable as absent information.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state a 35S::PbTPS overexpression cassette was used.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PbTPS-a21 is a TPS-a sesquiterpene synthase in terpenoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: It is directly involved in sesquiterpene scaffold formation.

Gene_Type.reason: It is directly assayed as a biosynthetic TPS gene.

Enzyme_Name.reason: It is functionally characterized as a sesquiterpene synthase.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is provided for PbTPS-a21 or TPS-a enzymes in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper states all 72 predicted TPS proteins contain typical TPS domains and identification used PF01397/PF03936 at family level, but does not explicitly annotate these PFAM domains for PbTPS-a21 individually.

Primary_Substrate.reason: As a sesquiterpene synthase, FPP is biologically inferable, but the paper does not explicitly state the substrate used for PbTPS-a21 in the assay.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper states PbTPS-a21 produced β-caryophyllene and guaia-6,9-diene as major products, and elemene and ylangene as minor products.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The product profile is supported, but the explicit substrate-to-product reaction wording with FPP is not directly stated.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: TPS genes catalyze scaffold-forming steps generating sesquiterpene products; this is directly inferable from the paper's description of TPS function.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PbTPS-a21 directly forms β-caryophyllene in the transient assay.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: β-caryophyllene is explicitly identified as a product of PbTPS-a21 and a major tissue metabolite.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: β-caryophyllene is explicitly a sesquiterpenoid terpenoid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: β-caryophyllene is reported among the most abundant sesquiterpenes in all studied tissues, especially stem, and specifically 21.06% in stem xylem.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: β-caryophyllene and the essential oil showed antifungal activity against three fungi, supporting pathogen defense relevance.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper explicitly identifies PbTPS-a21 as one of the main synthases for β-caryophyllene in stem xylem.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states PbTPS-a21, PbTPS-a25, and PbTPS-a26 showed divergent gene expression patterns, and TPS-a genes generally are highly expressed in roots/stems.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Product detection in N. benthamiana is supported, but 'high/constitutive expression in stem/root-associated tissues' is not shown specifically for PbTPS-a21 in the provided text, only generally for TPS-a genes and divergence among these paralogs.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Tissue context is relevant and the family was also profiled under drought/cold, though not necessarily specifically interpreted for PbTPS-a21.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: TPS proteins are described as gatekeepers of terpenoid diversity; the note that it is not explicitly rate-limiting matches the paper.

Omics_Data.reason: The BioProject IDs and GC-MS metabolomics are explicitly provided.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are directly supported by Figure 6 and accompanying text.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transient overexpression in N. benthamiana, GC-MS, authentic standards, tissue VOC profiling, and transcriptomics are described.

Other_Important_Info.reason: PbTPS-a21 is explicitly described as paralogous with PbTPS-a26 and as sub-/neofunctionalized relative to it in Discussion.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Potential engineering of β-caryophyllene for resistance is plausible from the findings, but direct engineering validation was not performed.

corrections.reason: No EC number is provided for PbTPS-a21 or TPS-a enzymes in the paper.
